## [00:00 - 03:07]

And that's the beauty of this. And this is really what we want to cover because, you know, in the business world, generally, there's a lot of emphasis on being a strong salesperson. Nor do I find that most entrepreneurs is sales, it's something that they really want to do or really learn how to do. Marketing is not persuasion and marketing is not manipulation. See, a lot of people have this presentation or this idea in their head that marketing is persuading somebody to do something that they don't want to do or manipulating a scenario for someone to take an action that they might not want to take. Now, sure, you might be able to do some of that if you really want and you're really skilled. I don't think that's a long term play for your business, but it is an option. ## [36:48 - 39:48]

The first way is through association. Okay, association. One of the quickest ways to create positioning in your marketplace is by associating with other people or brands who are already influencers in your space. Now, I'm going to throw some names here, and I'm going to name drop a little bit. I don't know if these names will make any sense to you, because you might not be in the space. But for example, one of my early on clients that we got to serve was a guy by the name of Joe Polish. Now, Joe Polish is the head of the 25k group. He's the head of Piranha Marketing, and he's one of like the most well connected entrepreneurs in the entire world. He connects people with Branson and a whole bunch of other things. And so when I started to serve Joe, we started to get some great results. And now I was associated with him as being the key leading marketing strategist and Facebook strategist in his business. What do you think started to happen? Right? All of a sudden, everybody started to see me as a higher level authority. I was moving up in the ladder of the chain because of my associations. Now, when it comes to association, you can do one of two things. You can pay for them, or you can earn them. An example of paying for them would be to join a particular mastermind group or pay for a mentorship or do a joint venture deal or do some sort of a business transaction with somebody. Or on the flip side, you can earn it by going out and serving these people and working your network and making introductions and creating relationships and that sort of thing. And then you can create that association. So way number one to go up the ladder is through association. And way number two is through what I call affiliation. I mean, think about this, and I hope you get it by just using this simple example. If I said that my work was published on CNN, Huffington Post, Forbes Magazine, Entrepreneur Magazine, Success Magazine, and like Fox News or whatever, that would position me very differently than if I was like featured on a mommy blog, right? I hope you're seeing this. So the affiliations that you have, the groups that you are part of, the people that you write for, the networks that you are connected with will help elevate your status and your marketplace. And then feel free to use those affiliations in some of your communications and marketing. So again, coming back to the main point here, this is what happens when you know what you do, you know who you serve, and you know where you stand. Essentially, you begin to create this velvet rope around you, and you're only beginning to let your dream clients in. So what you're going to have to start to get really used to is what I call, quote unquote, rejection marketing. And that is simply being strong enough in your position in your business to say no to the wrong people and only yes to the right people. And here's what's going to happen when you do that. Number one, when you say no to the wrong people, A, it's going to make sure that you only work with people you like. And we've all determined that

that's something that we want to do. But B, even more than that, it makes the people that you are already working with feel special. I hope you're understanding that. So what I'm essentially saying is be the Ferrari, not the Toyota. Okay, now there's nothing wrong with the Toyota at all. But if you think about this analogy, the Ferrari salesman works a lot less and gets paid a lot more than the Toyota dealer. The first part of the marketing triangle is market pulse, right? First and foremost, you want to know what is the pulse of your industry. I don't know if you remember, but I told you about Kellogg's, for example, who when they first started, we're spending millions marketing corn flakes. And then all of a sudden, the fiber craze hit the high fiber craze hit in the industry, they shifted their marketing dollars to all brand and raisin brand, which were very quiet brands before, and they saved their business and their business skyrocketed. Starwood Hotels was another example. They realized that travelers wanted three things, nice pillows, nice beds and high speed internet. And so they branded these three things as part of their business and their business took off. Someone at Toyota realized that gas prices were going up, that people were shifting towards a more green alternative. in your industry right now? Or in other words, what is the wave that you start to, you need to start riding, right? In fitness, as an example, it's like CrossFit.
